summary: Sprint 9 is security-positive overall. All endpoints in the new portfolio_holdings.py and portfolio_transactions.py correctly use Depends(get_current_user) and _assert_portfolio_owner/holding_owner for IDOR prevention. The analytics.py shim only re-exports — no new endpoints, no new attack surface. Dependency upgrades (starlette 1.0.0, requests 2.33.0) patch known CVEs (CVE-2026-25645). The KisCredentialsSection toast message change is cosmetic-only. No hardcoded secrets, no injection paths, no new data exposure issues found.

findings.title: portfolio_transactions.py delete_transaction does not check user ownership in one path
findings.detail: delete_transaction fetches the transaction, then fetches the portfolio, and calls _assert_portfolio_owner. This is correct. However if the portfolio is not found (line 131-134) it raises 404 — this is appropriate behavior and does not expose data.
findings.fix: No fix needed — current behavior is correct.
